abstract: Receiving a cancer diagnosis necessitates making high-stakes, complex decisions regarding treatment pathways. In this chapter, we detail an interdisciplinary research initiative designed to automatically generate personalized descriptions of treatment options to assist patients in this process. Our approach utilizes two extensive databases curated by the Netherlands Comprehensive Cancer Organisation (IKNL): the Netherlands Cancer Registry and the PROFILES dataset. By integrating these sources, we were able to extract tailored clinical data and outcomes for various cancer types. We then augmented these statistics with personalized context—presented through both verbal descriptors and patient narratives—to better facilitate shared decision-making. We conclude by evaluating the strengths and limitations of our methodology, discussing its generalizability to other health domains, and offering broader reflections on the project’s outcomes.
